# System Settings: Theme & Language

## Summary

Add a system settings feature to Changelogue that lets users switch between light/dark/system theme and English/Chinese language. Settings are accessed from the sidebar user menu and displayed in a modal dialog. All preferences persist in localStorage (per-device).

## Decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|----------|--------|-----------|
| Entry point | Sidebar user menu dropdown | Non-intrusive, near existing user profile section |
| Settings UI | Modal dialog | Lightweight for 2 settings, no page navigation needed |
| Theme options | Light / Dark / System | System follows OS `prefers-color-scheme`, default |
| Languages | English + Chinese (Simplified) | Two languages to start |
| Persistence | localStorage | No backend changes, per-device, simple |
| Theme library | next-themes | Handles `.dark` class, flash prevention, 3KB |
| i18n approach | Custom React context | Zero deps, simple for 2 languages, flat JSON keys |

## Architecture

```
Providers (root layout, nesting order matters)
ThemeProvider (outermost вАФ sets class on <html>)
вФФвФАвФА LanguageProvider
    вФФвФАвФА AuthProvider (existing, innermost)

Sidebar user menu
вФФвФАвФА DropdownMenu
    вФЬвФАвФА Settings вЖТ opens SettingsDialog
    вФФвФАвФА Sign out (existing)

SettingsDialog (modal)
вФЬвФАвФА Theme вАФ Light/Dark/System toggle buttons (sun/moon/monitor icons)
вФФвФАвФА Language вАФ Dropdown select (English / дЄ≠жЦЗ)
```

## New Files

### `web/lib/i18n/context.tsx`
- `LanguageProvider` вАФ reads `locale` from localStorage, default `en`
- `useTranslation()` hook вАФ returns `{ t, locale, setLocale }`
- `t(key)` вАФ looks up translation string from current locale's messages

### `web/lib/i18n/messages/en.json`
- Flat dot-notation keys: `"nav.dashboard": "Dashboard"`, etc.
- Covers: navigation labels, settings UI, page titles

### `web/lib/i18n/messages/zh.json`
- Same keys, Chinese translations

### `web/components/settings/settings-dialog.tsx`
- Modal dialog with two sections (no tabs)
- Theme: three toggle buttons with icons (Sun/Moon/Monitor)
- Language: Select dropdown (English / дЄ≠жЦЗ)
- Uses existing shadcn Dialog, Select components
- Theme controlled via `next-themes` `useTheme()`
- Language controlled via custom `useTranslation()`

## Modified Files

### `web/app/layout.tsx`
- Wrap children with `ThemeProvider` (from next-themes) and `LanguageProvider`
- ThemeProvider config: `attribute="class"`, `defaultTheme="system"`, `enableSystem`

### `web/app/globals.css`
- Add `.dark` selector block with dark mode CSS variables:
  - `--background: #0f1115`
  - `--surface: #16181c`
  - `--foreground: #e5e7eb`
  - `--border: #2a2d33`
  - `--sidebar-bg: #0a0b0e`
  - `--text-muted: #6b7280` (same)
  - `--beacon-accent: #e8601a` (same)
  - Plus all shadcn token overrides (primary, secondary, card, popover, etc.)

### `web/components/layout/sidebar.tsx`
- Replace hardcoded user section with DropdownMenu
- Menu items: "Settings" (opens SettingsDialog), "Sign out" (existing)
- Import and render SettingsDialog

### `web/package.json`
- Add `next-themes` dependency

## Dark Mode Color Tokens

| Token | Light | Dark |
|-------|-------|------|
| --background | #fafaf9 | #0f1115 |
| --surface | #ffffff | #16181c |
| --foreground | #111113 | #e5e7eb |
| --border | #e8e8e5 | #2a2d33 |
| --sidebar-bg | #16181c | #0a0b0e |
| --beacon-accent | #e8601a | #e8601a |
| --text-muted | #6b7280 | #6b7280 |

## i18n Message Keys (initial set)

Translations cover navigation, settings UI, and page titles. All other UI text remains English initially and gets translated incrementally.

```json
{
  "nav.dashboard": "Dashboard",
  "nav.projects": "Projects",
  "nav.todo": "Todo",
  "nav.releases": "Releases",
  "nav.channels": "Channels",
  "nav.subscriptions": "Subscriptions",
  "settings.title": "Settings",
  "settings.appearance": "Appearance",
  "settings.theme": "Theme",
  "settings.theme.light": "Light",
  "settings.theme.dark": "Dark",
  "settings.theme.system": "System",
  "settings.language": "Language",
  "settings.language.en": "English",
  "settings.language.zh": "дЄ≠жЦЗ",
  "user.signout": "Sign out"
}
```

## Out of Scope

- Backend persistence (user_preferences API)
- RTL language support
- Additional languages beyond EN/ZH
- Translating dynamic content (release notes, agent reports)
- Per-page/component translation beyond nav, settings, and page titles

# System Settings: Theme & Language

## Summary

Add a system settings feature to Changelogue that lets users switch between light/dark/system theme and English/Chinese language. Settings are accessed from the sidebar user menu and displayed in a modal dialog. All preferences persist in localStorage (per-device).

## Decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|----------|--------|-----------|
| Entry point | Sidebar user menu dropdown | Non-intrusive, near existing user profile section |
| Settings UI | Modal dialog | Lightweight for 2 settings, no page navigation needed |
| Theme options | Light / Dark / System | System follows OS `prefers-color-scheme`, default |
| Languages | English + Chinese (Simplified) | Two languages to start |
| Persistence | localStorage | No backend changes, per-device, simple |
| Theme library | next-themes | Handles `.dark` class, flash prevention, 3KB |
| i18n approach | Custom React context | Zero deps, simple for 2 languages, flat JSON keys |

## Architecture

```
Providers (root layout, nesting order matters)
ThemeProvider (outermost вАФ sets class on <html>)
вФФвФАвФА LanguageProvider
    вФФвФАвФА AuthProvider (existing, innermost)

Sidebar user menu
вФФвФАвФА DropdownMenu
    вФЬвФАвФА Settings вЖТ opens SettingsDialog
    вФФвФАвФА Sign out (existing)

SettingsDialog (modal)
вФЬвФАвФА Theme вАФ Light/Dark/System toggle buttons (sun/moon/monitor icons)
вФФвФАвФА Language вАФ Dropdown select (English / дЄ≠жЦЗ)
```

## New Files

### `web/lib/i18n/context.tsx`
- `LanguageProvider` вАФ reads `locale` from localStorage key `"changelogue-locale"`, default `en`
- On locale change, updates `document.documentElement.lang` to keep `<html lang>` in sync
- `useTranslation()` hook вАФ returns `{ t, locale, setLocale }`
- `t(key)` вАФ looks up translation string from current locale's messages. Falls back to English string if key missing in current locale. Returns the key itself if missing from all locales.

### `web/lib/i18n/messages/en.json`
- Flat dot-notation keys: `"nav.dashboard": "Dashboard"`, etc.
- Covers: navigation labels, settings UI, page titles

### `web/lib/i18n/messages/zh.json`
- Same keys, Chinese translations

### `web/components/settings/settings-dialog.tsx`
- Modal dialog with two sections (no tabs)
- Theme: three toggle buttons with icons (Sun/Moon/Monitor)
- Language: Select dropdown (English / дЄ≠жЦЗ)
- Uses existing shadcn Dialog, Select components
- Theme controlled via `next-themes` `useTheme()`
- Language controlled via custom `useTranslation()`

## Modified Files

### `web/app/layout.tsx`
- Add `suppressHydrationWarning` to `<html>` element (required by next-themes)
- Wrap children: `<ThemeProvider><LanguageProvider><AuthProvider>...</AuthProvider></LanguageProvider></ThemeProvider>`
- ThemeProvider config: `attribute="class"`, `defaultTheme="system"`, `enableSystem`, `storageKey="changelogue-theme"`

### `web/app/globals.css`
- Add `html.dark` selector block (plain CSS, not Tailwind variant) with dark mode CSS variables:
  - `--background: #0f1115`
  - `--surface: #16181c`
  - `--foreground: #e5e7eb`
  - `--border: #2a2d33`
  - `--sidebar-bg: #0a0b0e`
  - `--text-muted: #6b7280` (same)
  - `--beacon-accent: #e8601a` (same)
  - Plus all shadcn token overrides (primary, secondary, card, popover, etc.)

### `web/components/layout/sidebar.tsx`
- Replace hardcoded user section with DropdownMenu
- Menu items: "Settings" (opens SettingsDialog), "Sign out" (existing)
- Import and render SettingsDialog
- Refactor hardcoded inline color styles (e.g., `backgroundColor: "#16181c"`) to use CSS variables so dark mode applies correctly
- In collapsed mode (52px), the avatar serves as the DropdownMenu trigger with `side="right"` positioning

### `web/package.json`
- Add `next-themes` dependency

## Dark Mode Color Tokens

| Token | Light | Dark |
|-------|-------|------|
| --background | #fafaf9 | #0f1115 |
| --surface | #ffffff | #16181c |
| --foreground | #111113 | #e5e7eb |
| --border | #e8e8e5 | #2a2d33 |
| --sidebar-bg | #16181c | #0a0b0e |
| --beacon-accent | #e8601a | #e8601a |
| --text-muted | #6b7280 | #6b7280 |

## i18n Message Keys (initial set)

Translations cover navigation, settings UI, and page titles. All other UI text remains English initially and gets translated incrementally.

```json
{
  "nav.dashboard": "Dashboard",
  "nav.projects": "Projects",
  "nav.todo": "Todo",
  "nav.releases": "Releases",
  "nav.channels": "Channels",
  "nav.subscriptions": "Subscriptions",
  "settings.title": "Settings",
  "settings.appearance": "Appearance",
  "settings.theme": "Theme",
  "settings.theme.light": "Light",
  "settings.theme.dark": "Dark",
  "settings.theme.system": "System",
  "settings.language": "Language",
  "settings.language.en": "English",
  "settings.language.zh": "дЄ≠жЦЗ",
  "user.signout": "Sign out"
}
```

## Out of Scope

- Backend persistence (user_preferences API)
- RTL language support
- Additional languages beyond EN/ZH
- Translating dynamic content (release notes, agent reports)
- Per-page/component translation beyond nav, settings, and page titles

lines: - Translating dynamic content (release notes, agent reports)
- Per-page/component translation beyond nav, settings, and page titles

+- Refactoring all hardcoded colors across the codebase (components outside sidebar that use inline hex colors or Tailwind arbitrary values like `bg-[#f3f3f1]` will not respond to dark mode вАФ these get fixed incrementally in follow-up work)
+- Dark mode overrides for `.release-notes-content` CSS block
+- Login/onboard page dark mode styling (these pages use hardcoded colors and will be addressed separately)

Base directory for this skill: /Users/pc/.claude/plugins/cache/superpowers-marketplace/superpowers/5.0.5/skills/writing-plans

# Writing Plans

## Overview

Write comprehensive implementation plans assuming the engineer has zero context for our codebase and questionable taste. Document everything they need to know: which files to touch for each task, code, testing, docs they might need to check, how to test it. Give them the whole plan as bite-sized tasks. DRY. YAGNI. TDD. Frequent commits.

Assume they are a skilled developer, but know almost nothing about our toolset or problem domain. Assume they don't know good test design very well.

**Announce at start:** "I'm using the writing-plans skill to create the implementation plan."

**Context:** This should be run in a dedicated worktree (created by brainstorming skill).

**Save plans to:** `docs/superpowers/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<feature-name>.md`
- (User preferences for plan location override this default)

## Scope Check

If the spec covers multiple independent subsystems, it should have been broken into sub-project specs during brainstorming. If it wasn't, suggest breaking this into separate plans вАФ one per subsystem. Each plan should produce working, testable software on its own.

## File Structure

Before defining tasks, map out which files will be created or modified and what each one is responsible for. This is where decomposition decisions get locked in.

- Design units with clear boundaries and well-defined interfaces. Each file should have one clear responsibility.
- You reason best about code you can hold in context at once, and your edits are more reliable when files are focused. Prefer smaller, focused files over large ones that do too much.
- Files that change together should live together. Split by responsibility, not by technical layer.
- In existing codebases, follow established patterns. If the codebase uses large files, don't unilaterally restructure - but if a file you're modifying has grown unwieldy, including a split in the plan is reasonable.

This structure informs the task decomposition. Each task should produce self-contained changes that make sense independently.

## Bite-Sized Task Granularity

**Each step is one action (2-5 minutes):**
- "Write the failing test" - step
- "Run it to make sure it fails" - step
- "Implement the minimal code to make the test pass" - step
- "Run the tests and make sure they pass" - step
- "Commit" - step

## Plan Document Header

**Every plan MUST start with this header:**

```markdown
# [Feature Name] Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** [One sentence describing what this builds]

**Architecture:** [2-3 sentences about approach]

**Tech Stack:** [Key technologies/libraries]

---
```

## Task Structure

````markdown
### Task N: [Component Name]

**Files:**
- Create: `exact/path/to/file.py`
- Modify: `exact/path/to/existing.py:123-145`
- Test: `tests/exact/path/to/test.py`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing test**

```python
def test_specific_behavior():
    result = function(input)
    assert result == expected
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run test to verify it fails**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_name -v`
Expected: FAIL with "function not defined"

- [ ] **Step 3: Write minimal implementation**

```python
def function(input):
    return expected
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Run test to verify it passes**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_name -v`
Expected: PASS

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add tests/path/test.py src/path/file.py
git commit -m "feat: add specific feature"
```
````

## Remember
- Exact file paths always
- Complete code in plan (not "add validation")
- Exact commands with expected output
- Reference relevant skills with @ syntax
- DRY, YAGNI, TDD, frequent commits

## Plan Review Loop

After writing the complete plan:

1. Dispatch a single plan-document-reviewer subagent (see plan-document-reviewer-prompt.md) with precisely crafted review context вАФ never your session history. This keeps the reviewer focused on the plan, not your thought process.
   - Provide: path to the plan document, path to spec document
2. If вЭМ Issues Found: fix the issues, re-dispatch reviewer for the whole plan
3. If вЬЕ Approved: proceed to execution handoff

**Review loop guidance:**
- Same agent that wrote the plan fixes it (preserves context)
- If loop exceeds 3 iterations, surface to human for guidance
- Reviewers are advisory вАФ explain disagreements if you believe feedback is incorrect

## Execution Handoff

After saving the plan, offer execution choice:

**"Plan complete and saved to `docs/superpowers/plans/<filename>.md`. Two execution options:**

**1. Subagent-Driven (recommended)** - I dispatch a fresh subagent per task, review between tasks, fast iteration

**2. Inline Execution** - Execute tasks in this session using executing-plans, batch execution with checkpoints

**Which approach?"**

**If Subagent-Driven chosen:**
- **REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development
- Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review

**If Inline Execution chosen:**
- **REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** Use superpowers:executing-plans
- Batch execution with checkpoints for review

I'm using the writing-plans skill to create the implementation plan.

Let me read the spec and explore the key files that will be modified to write precise implementation steps.

- Refactor hardcoded inline color styles (e.g., `backgroundColor: "#16181c"`) to use CSS variables so dark mode applies correctly
- In collapsed mode (52px), the avatar serves as the DropdownMenu trigger with `side="right"` positioning

### `web/package.json`
- Add `next-themes` dependency

## Dark Mode Color Tokens

| Token | Light | Dark |
|-------|-------|------|
| --background | #fafaf9 | #0f1115 |
| --surface | #ffffff | #16181c |
| --foreground | #111113 | #e5e7eb |
| --border | #e8e8e5 | #2a2d33 |
| --sidebar-bg | #16181c | #0a0b0e |
| --beacon-accent | #e8601a | #e8601a |
| --text-muted | #6b7280 | #6b7280 |

## i18n Message Keys (initial set)

Translations cover navigation, settings UI, and page titles. All other UI text remains English initially and gets translated incrementally.

```json
{
  "nav.dashboard": "Dashboard",
  "nav.projects": "Projects",
  "nav.todo": "Todo",
  "nav.releases": "Releases",
  "nav.channels": "Channels",
  "nav.subscriptions": "Subscriptions",
  "settings.title": "Settings",
  "settings.appearance": "Appearance",
  "settings.theme": "Theme",
  "settings.theme.light": "Light",
  "settings.theme.dark": "Dark",
  "settings.theme.system": "System",
  "settings.language": "Language",
  "settings.language.en": "English",
  "settings.language.zh": "дЄ≠жЦЗ",
  "user.signout": "Sign out"
}
```

## Out of Scope

- Backend persistence (user_preferences API)
- RTL language support
- Additional languages beyond EN/ZH
- Translating dynamic content (release notes, agent reports)
- Per-page/component translation beyond nav, settings, and page titles
- Refactoring all hardcoded colors across the codebase (components outside sidebar that use inline hex colors or Tailwind arbitrary values like `bg-[#f3f3f1]` will not respond to dark mode вАФ these get fixed incrementally in follow-up work)
- Dark mode overrides for `.release-notes-content` CSS block
- Login/onboard page dark mode styling (these pages use hardcoded colors and will be addressed separately)

1вЖТ# System Settings (Theme & Language) Implementation Plan 2вЖТ 3вЖТ> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking. 4вЖТ 5вЖТ**Goal:** Add a settings dialog accessible from the sidebar user menu that lets users toggle light/dark/system theme and switch between English and Chinese. 6вЖТ 7вЖТ**Architecture:** `next-themes` ThemeProvider + custom LanguageProvider context wrap the app. Settings dialog uses existing shadcn Dialog/DropdownMenu/Select components. All preferences persist in localStorage. 8вЖТ 9вЖТ**Tech Stack:** Next.js 16 (App Router), next-themes, React Context, Tailwind CSS v4, shadcn/ui, Lucide icons 10вЖТ 11вЖТ**Spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-23-system-settings-design.md` 12вЖТ 13вЖТ--- 14вЖТ 15вЖТ## File Structure 16вЖТ 17вЖТ| File | Action | Responsibility | 18вЖТ|------|--------|---------------| 19вЖТ| `web/lib/i18n/context.tsx` | Create | LanguageProvider + useTranslation hook | 20вЖТ| `web/lib/i18n/messages/en.json` | Create | English translation strings | 21вЖТ| `web/lib/i18n/messages/zh.json` | Create | Chinese translation strings | 22вЖТ| `web/components/settings/settings-dialog.tsx` | Create | Settings modal UI | 23вЖТ| `web/app/globals.css` | Modify | Add `html.dark` CSS variables block | 24вЖТ| `web/app/layout.tsx` | Modify | Add ThemeProvider + LanguageProvider + suppressHydrationWarning | 25вЖТ| `web/components/layout/sidebar.tsx` | Modify | Replace user section with DropdownMenu, content.content:
1вЖТ# System Settings: Theme & Language
     2вЖТ
     3вЖТ## Summary
     4вЖТ
     5вЖТAdd a system settings feature to Changelogue that lets users switch between light/dark/system theme and English/Chinese language. Settings are accessed from the sidebar user menu and displayed in a modal dialog. All preferences persist in localStorage (per-device).
     6вЖТ
     7вЖТ## Decisions
     8вЖТ
     9вЖТ| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
    10вЖТ|----------|--------|-----------|
    11вЖТ| Entry point | Sidebar user menu dropdown | Non-intrusive, near existing user profile section |
    12вЖТ| Settings UI | Modal dialog | Lightweight for 2 settings, no page navigation needed |
    13вЖТ| Theme options | Light / Dark / System | System follows OS `prefers-color-scheme`, default |
    14вЖТ| Languages | English + Chinese (Simplified) | Two languages to start |
    15вЖТ| Persistence | localStorage | No backend changes, per-device, simple |
    16вЖТ| Theme library | next-themes | Handles `.dark` class, flash prevention, 3KB |
    17вЖТ| i18n approach | Custom React context | Zero deps, simple for 2 languages, flat JSON keys |
    18вЖТ
    19вЖТ## Architecture
    20вЖТ
    21вЖТ```
    22вЖТProviders (root layout, nesting order matters)
    23вЖТThemeProvider (outermost вАФ sets class on <html>)
    24вЖТвФФвФАвФА LanguageProvider
    25вЖТ    вФФвФАвФА AuthProvider (existing, innermost)
    26вЖТ
    27вЖТSidebar user menu
    28вЖТвФФвФАвФА DropdownMenu
    29вЖТ    вФЬвФАвФА Settings вЖТ opens SettingsDialog
    30вЖТ    вФФвФАвФА Sign out (existing)
    31вЖТ
    32вЖТSettingsDialog (modal)
    33вЖТвФЬвФАвФА Theme вАФ Light/Dark/System toggle buttons (sun/moon/monitor icons)
    34вЖТвФФвФАвФА Language вАФ Dropdown select (English / дЄ≠жЦЗ)
    35вЖТ```
    36вЖТ
    37вЖТ## New Files
    38вЖТ
    39вЖТ### `web/lib/i18n/context.tsx`
    40вЖТ- `LanguageProvider` вАФ reads `locale` from localStorage key `"changelogue-locale"`, default `en`
    41вЖТ- On locale change, updates `document.documentElement.lang` to keep `<html lang>` in sync
    42вЖТ- `useTranslation()` hook вАФ returns `{ t, locale, setLocale }`
    43вЖТ- `t(key)` вАФ looks up translation string from current locale's messages. Falls back to English string if key missing in current locale. Returns the key itself if missing from all locales.
    44вЖТ
    45вЖТ### `web/lib/i18n/messages/en.json`
    46вЖТ- Flat dot-notation keys: `"nav.dashboard": "Dashboard"`, etc.
    47вЖТ- Covers: navigation labels, settings UI, page titles
    48вЖТ
    49вЖТ### `web/lib/i18n/messages/zh.json`
    50вЖТ- Same keys, Chinese translations
    51вЖТ
    52вЖТ### `web/components/settings/settings-dialog.tsx`
    53вЖТ- Modal dialog with two sections (no tabs)
    54вЖТ- Theme: three toggle buttons with icons (Sun/Moon/Monitor)
    55вЖТ- Language: Select dropdown (English / дЄ≠жЦЗ)
    56вЖТ- Uses existing shadcn Dialog, Select components
    57вЖТ- Theme controlled via `next-themes` `useTheme()`
    58вЖТ- Language controlled via custom `useTranslation()`
    59вЖТ
    60вЖТ## Modified Files
    61вЖТ
    62вЖТ### `web/app/layout.tsx`
    63вЖТ- Add `suppressHydrationWarning` to `<html>` element (required by next-themes)
    64вЖТ- Wrap children: `<ThemeProvider><LanguageProvider><AuthProvider>...</AuthProvider></LanguageProvider></ThemeProvider>`
    65вЖТ- ThemeProvider config: `attribute="class"`, `defaultTheme="system"`, `enableSystem`, `storageKey="changelogue-theme"`
    66вЖТ
    67вЖТ### `web/app/globals.css`
    68вЖТ- Add `html.dark` selector block (plain CSS, not Tailwind variant) with dark mode CSS variables:
    69вЖТ  - `--background: #0f1115`
    70вЖТ  - `--surface: #16181c`
    71вЖТ  - `--foreground: #e5e7eb`
    72вЖТ  - `--border: #2a2d33`
    73вЖТ  - `--sidebar-bg: #0a0b0e`
    74вЖТ  - `--text-muted: #6b7280` (same)
    75вЖТ  - `--beacon-accent: #e8601a` (same)
    76вЖТ  - Plus all shadcn token overrides (primary, secondary, card, popover, etc.)
    77вЖТ
    78вЖТ### `web/components/layout/sidebar.tsx`
    79вЖТ- Replace hardcoded user section with DropdownMenu
    80вЖТ- Menu items: "Settings" (opens SettingsDialog), "Sign out" (existing)
    81вЖТ- Import and render SettingsDialog
    82вЖТ- Refactor hardcoded inline color styles (e.g., `backgroundColor: "#16181c"`) to use CSS variables so dark mode applies correctly
    83вЖТ- In collapsed mode (52px), the avatar serves as the DropdownMenu trigger with `side="right"` positioning
    84вЖТ
    85вЖТ### `web/package.json`
    86вЖТ- Add `next-themes` dependency
    87вЖТ
    88вЖТ## Dark Mode Color Tokens
    89вЖТ
    90вЖТ| Token | Light | Dark |
    91вЖТ|-------|-------|------|
    92вЖТ| --background | #fafaf9 | #0f1115 |
    93вЖТ| --surface | #ffffff | #16181c |
    94вЖТ| --foreground | #111113 | #e5e7eb |
    95вЖТ| --border | #e8e8e5 | #2a2d33 |
    96вЖТ| --sidebar-bg | #16181c | #0a0b0e |
    97вЖТ| --beacon-accent | #e8601a | #e8601a |
    98вЖТ| --text-muted | #6b7280 | #6b7280 |
    99вЖТ
   100вЖТ## i18n Message Keys (initial set)
   101вЖТ
   102вЖТTranslations cover navigation, settings UI, and page titles. All other UI text remains English initially and gets translated incrementally.
   103вЖТ
   104вЖТ```json
   105вЖТ{
   106вЖТ  "nav.dashboard": "Dashboard",
   107вЖТ  "nav.projects": "Projects",
   108вЖТ  "nav.todo": "Todo",
   109вЖТ  "nav.releases": "Releases",
   110вЖТ  "nav.channels": "Channels",
   111вЖТ  "nav.subscriptions": "Subscriptions",
   112вЖТ  "settings.title": "Settings",
   113вЖТ  "settings.appearance": "Appearance",
   114вЖТ  "settings.theme": "Theme",
   115вЖТ  "settings.theme.light": "Light",
   116вЖТ  "settings.theme.dark": "Dark",
   117вЖТ  "settings.theme.system": "System",
   118вЖТ  "settings.language": "Language",
   119вЖТ  "settings.language.en": "English",
   120вЖТ  "settings.language.zh": "дЄ≠жЦЗ",
   121вЖТ  "user.signout": "Sign out"
   122вЖТ}
   123вЖТ```
   124вЖТ
   125вЖТ## Out of Scope
   126вЖТ
   127вЖТ- Backend persistence (user_preferences API)
   128вЖТ- RTL language support
   129вЖТ- Additional languages beyond EN/ZH
   130вЖТ- Translating dynamic content (release notes, agent reports)
   131вЖТ- Per-page/component translation beyond nav, settings, and page titles
   132вЖТ- Refactoring all hardcoded colors across the codebase (components outside sidebar that use inline hex colors or Tailwind arbitrary values like `bg-[#f3f3f1]` will not respond to dark mode вАФ these get fixed incrementally in follow-up work)
   133вЖТ- Dark mode overrides for `.release-notes-content` CSS block
   134вЖТ- Login/onboard page dark mode styling (these pages use hardcoded colors and will be addressed separately)
   135вЖТ

content.text:
Now I have all the information needed to perform a thorough review. Let me analyze systematically.

---

## ISSUES FOUND

After reviewing the implementation plan against the design spec and the existing codebase, I identified the following issues:

### 1. ThemeProvider cannot be used directly in a Server Component layout

`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/app/layout.tsx` is a **Server Component** (no `"use client"` directive). The plan (Task 4) imports `ThemeProvider` from `next-themes` and `LanguageProvider` (a client component) directly into the Server Component layout and uses them as JSX wrappers. This will fail at build time because `next-themes`'s `ThemeProvider` is a Client Component.

**Fix:** Create a small wrapper client component (e.g., `web/components/providers.tsx` with `"use client"`) that composes `ThemeProvider` and `LanguageProvider`, then import that single `<Providers>` component into `layout.tsx`. Alternatively, wrap just the `ThemeProvider` import with a `"use client"` barrel file. This is a standard Next.js App Router pattern.

### 2. Spec has `"settings.appearance"` key but plan omits it

The design spec's i18n keys (line 113) include `"settings.appearance": "Appearance"`, but the plan's `en.json` and `zh.json` in Task 3 do not include this key. While the settings dialog in Task 5 does not currently use it (it only uses `"settings.theme"` and `"settings.language"`), the plan should either include this key for spec fidelity or explicitly note its removal. This is a minor gap.

### 3. Spec has `"user.settings"` key but the spec's key list does not include it

Inversely, the plan's `en.json` includes `"user.settings": "Settings"` and `"user.signout": "Sign out"`, but the spec's key list only shows `"user.signout"` -- it is missing `"user.settings"`. The plan is actually **more correct** than the spec here (the dropdown menu needs the "Settings" label). This is fine in the plan, but worth noting the spec discrepancy.

### 4. Nav items refactor in Task 6 Step 4 is incomplete -- `navItems` is used in two ways

In the existing sidebar (line 75-110 of `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/components/layout/sidebar.tsx`), the nav items array is defined at **module scope** (line 20-27) as a `const navItems`. The plan renames this to `navKeys` with a `key` property instead of `label`, and says to change `item.label` to `t(item.key)`. However:

- `navKeys` uses `t()` which requires the `useTranslation()` hook, meaning `navKeys` can no longer be a module-level constant. It must be moved inside the component body (after the `const { t } = useTranslation()` call), or the array must remain with static `key` strings at module level and `t()` is only called in the JSX. The plan's Step 4 says to "Replace the `navItems` const" but does not clarify that the new `navKeys` array should still remain at module scope (it can, since it only contains string keys, not translated values). The JSX mapping must change from `item.label` to `t(item.key)`. This should be made explicit: the `navKeys` const stays at module scope, and only the rendering calls `t()`.

- The plan says to update "two places: the `title` attribute and the `<span>` text" but in the existing code, the `title` attribute uses `item.label` (line 84), and the span uses `item.label` (line 106). Both need to change to `t(item.key)`. This is stated but the exact code diff is not provided in Task 6 Step 4, unlike all other steps which provide exact code. **This step needs a concrete code snippet** showing the nav mapping changes.

### 5. Missing `useState` import update in Task 6

Task 6 Step 2 adds `const [settingsOpen, setSettingsOpen] = useState(false);` inside the Sidebar component. The existing sidebar already imports `useState` (line 3), so this is fine. However, this is worth confirming -- the plan does not explicitly state "useState is already imported, no change needed." If an implementor misses this, they might add a duplicate import.

### 6. `--sidebar-bg` variable change in Task 6 Step 3 has a dark-mode concern

The plan changes `backgroundColor: "#16181c"` to `backgroundColor: "var(--sidebar-bg)"`. In the `:root` block, `--sidebar-bg: #16181c` (the dark sidebar on light theme). In `html.dark`, it becomes `--sidebar-bg: #0a0b0e` (even darker). This is correct per the spec's color tokens table. No issue here -- just confirming alignment.

### 7. CSS variable placement instruction is fragile

Task 2 Step 1 says "Insert this block right after the closing `}` of `:root` (after line 79)." The `:root` block indeed ends at line 79 in the current `globals.css`. However, line references are fragile if any earlier tasks modify the file. Since Task 2 is the only task that modifies `globals.css`, and Task 1 only touches `package.json`, this is acceptable. No issue.

### 8. `--destructive` variable missing from `html.dark` block

Task 2 Step 2 changes `--color-destructive: #dc2626` to `--color-destructive: var(--destructive, #dc2626)` in the `@theme inline` block. However, the `html.dark` CSS variable block in Task 2 Step 1 does **not** define a `--destructive` variable. This means the fallback `#dc2626` will always be used (same in both themes). This is fine for now (spec says destructive stays the same), but the pattern of using `var(--destructive, #dc2626)` implies the variable might be overridden later. This is a minor cleanliness issue -- either add `--destructive: #dc2626` to the `html.dark` block for consistency, or keep the hardcoded value in `@theme inline` and skip the `var()` wrapping. Not a blocker.

### 9. Missing `--sidebar-text` from `html.dark` block

The `:root` block defines `--sidebar-text: #9ca3af` (line 51), and the `@theme inline` block maps it to `--color-sidebar-text` (line 35). The `html.dark` block in Task 2 does **not** define `--sidebar-text`. This means in dark mode, `--sidebar-text` will inherit the `:root` value `#9ca3af`. Looking at the spec's color table, `--text-muted` stays the same in both themes, so presumably sidebar-text also stays the same. This is fine but should be explicitly noted for completeness. Not a blocker.

### 10. Theme comparison in SettingsDialog uses `theme` which can be "system"

In the SettingsDialog (Task 5), the active state check is `theme === opt.value`. When the user selects "system", `useTheme()` returns `theme: "system"` and `resolvedTheme: "light"` or `"dark"`. This is correct -- the button highlight will show "System" as active when `theme === "system"`. No issue.

---

## Summary of Blocking Issues

Only **Issue #1** is a blocking correctness issue that will cause a build failure. The rest are minor gaps or documentation clarity issues.

**Verdict: ISSUES FOUND**

1. **(BLOCKING)** Task 4 imports `ThemeProvider` and `LanguageProvider` (Client Components) directly in the Server Component `layout.tsx`. This will fail. Create a `"use client"` wrapper component (e.g., `web/components/providers.tsx`) that composes both providers, then import that into `layout.tsx`.
2. **(Minor)** Spec key `"settings.appearance"` is not included in the translation JSON files. Add it or document its removal.
3. **(Minor)** Task 6 Step 4 lacks a concrete code snippet for the nav items mapping change. All other steps provide exact code diffs; this one should too.
4. **(Minor)** Task 2 introduces `var(--destructive, #dc2626)` but never defines `--destructive` in the `html.dark` block. Either define it or keep the hardcoded value.

1вЖТ# System Settings (Theme & Language) Implementation Plan 2вЖТ 3вЖТ> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking. 4вЖТ 5вЖТ**Goal:** Add a settings dialog accessible from the sidebar user menu that lets users toggle light/dark/system theme and switch between English and Chinese. 6вЖТ 7вЖТ**Architecture:** `next-themes` ThemeProvider + custom LanguageProvider context wrap the app. Settings dialog uses existing shadcn Dialog/DropdownMenu/Select components. All preferences persist in localStorage. 8вЖТ 9вЖТ**Tech Stack:** Next.js 16 (App Router), next-themes, React Context, Tailwind CSS v4, shadcn/ui, Lucide icons 10вЖТ 11вЖТ**Spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-23-system-settings-design.md` 12вЖТ 13вЖТ--- 14вЖТ 15вЖТ## File Structure 16вЖТ 17вЖТ| File | Action | Responsibility | 18вЖТ|------|--------|---------------| 19вЖТ| `web/lib/i18n/context.tsx` | Create | LanguageProvider + useTranslation hook | 20вЖТ| `web/lib/i18n/messages/en.json` | Create | English translation strings | 21вЖТ| `web/lib/i18n/messages/zh.json` | Create | Chinese translation strings | 22вЖТ| `web/components/settings/settings-dialog.tsx` | Create | Settings modal UI | 23вЖТ| `web/app/globals.css` | Modify | Add `html.dark` CSS variables block | 24вЖТ| `web/app/layout.tsx` | Modify | Add ThemeProvider + LanguageProvider + suppressHydrationWarning | 25вЖТ| `web/components/layout/sidebar.tsx` | Modify | Replace user section with DropdownMenu, refactor hardcoded colors | 26вЖТ 27вЖТ--- 28вЖТ 29вЖТ### Task 1: Install next-themes 30вЖТ 31вЖТ**Files:** 32вЖТ- Modify: `web/package.json` 33вЖТ 34вЖТ- [ ] **Step 1: Install the package** 35вЖТ 36вЖТ```bash 37вЖТcd web && npm install next-themes 38вЖТ``` 39вЖТ 40вЖТ- [ ] **Step 2: Verify installation** 41вЖТ 42вЖТ```bash 43вЖТcd web && node -e "require('next-themes')" && echo "OK" 44вЖТ``` 45вЖТExpected: `OK` 46вЖТ 47вЖТ- [ ] **Step 3: Commit** 48вЖТ 49вЖТ```bash 50вЖТgit add web/package.json web/package-lock.json 51вЖТgit commit -m "chore: add next-themes dependency" 52вЖТ``` 53вЖТ (after line 79)** 62вЖТ 63вЖТInsert this block right after the closing `}` of `:root`: 64вЖТ 65вЖТ```css 66вЖТhtml.dark { 67вЖТ --background: #0f1115; 68вЖТ --foreground: #e5e7eb; 69вЖТ --surface: --primary: #e8601a; 84вЖТ --primary-foreground: #ffffff; 85вЖТ --secondary: #1e2025; 86вЖТ --secondary-foreground: #d1d5db; 87вЖТ --muted: #1e2025; 88вЖТ --muted-foreground: #9ca3af; 89вЖТ --accent: import { AuthProvider } from "@/lib/auth/context";

Add these imports after the existing ones:
```tsx
import { ThemeProvider } from "next-themes";

```

Change the `<html>` tag from:
```tsx

to:

          <LayoutShell>{children}</LayoutShell>
        </AuthProvider>

to:

        <ThemeProvider
          attribute="class"
          defaultTheme="system"

### Task 4: Create Providers wrapper and wire into root layout

**Files:**
- Create: `web/components/providers.tsx`
- Modify: `web/app/layout.tsx`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create the client-side Providers wrapper**

`layout.tsx` is a Server Component and cannot directly use Client Components like `ThemeProvider` or `LanguageProvider` as JSX. Create a wrapper.

Create `web/components/providers.tsx`:

```tsx
"use client";

import { ThemeProvider } from "next-themes";
import { LanguageProvider } from "@/lib/i18n/context";
import type { ReactNode } from "react";

export function Providers({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) {
  return (
    <ThemeProvider
      attribute="class"
      defaultTheme="system"
      enableSystem
      storageKey="changelogue-theme"
    >
      <LanguageProvider>{children}</LanguageProvider>
    </ThemeProvider>
  );
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Update layout.tsx**

Replace the import:
```tsx
import { AuthProvider } from "@/lib/auth/context";
```

Add after it:
```tsx
import { Providers } from "@/components/providers";
```

Change the `<html>` tag from:
```tsx
    <html lang="en">
```
to:
```tsx
    <html lang="en" suppressHydrationWarning>
```

Change the body content from:
```tsx
        <AuthProvider>
          <LayoutShell>{children}</LayoutShell>
        </AuthProvider>
```
to:
```tsx
        <Providers>
          <AuthProvider>
            <LayoutShell>{children}</LayoutShell>
          </AuthProvider>
        </Providers>
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify the dev server starts**

```bash
cd web && npx next build 2>&1 | tail -5
```
Expected: Build succeeds.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add web/components/providers.tsx web/app/layout.tsx
git commit -m "feat: wire ThemeProvider and LanguageProvider into root layout via Providers wrapper"
```

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/pc/.claude/plugins/cache/superpowers-marketplace/superpowers/5.0.5/skills/subagent-driven-development

# Subagent-Driven Development

Execute plan by dispatching fresh subagent per task, with two-stage review after each: spec compliance review first, then code quality review.

**Why subagents:** You delegate tasks to specialized agents with isolated context. By precisely crafting their instructions and context, you ensure they stay focused and succeed at their task. They should never inherit your session's context or history вАФ you construct exactly what they need. This also preserves your own context for coordination work.

**Core principle:** Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review (spec then quality) = high quality, fast iteration

## When to Use

```dot
digraph when_to_use {
    "Have implementation plan?" [shape=diamond];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" [shape=diamond];
    "Stay in this session?" [shape=diamond];
    "subagent-driven-development" [shape=box];
    "executing-plans" [shape=box];
    "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [shape=box];

    "Have implementation plan?" -> "Tasks mostly independent?" [label="yes"];
    "Have implementation plan?" -> "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [label="no"];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" -> "Stay in this session?" [label="yes"];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" -> "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [label="no - tightly coupled"];
    "Stay in this session?" -> "subagent-driven-development" [label="yes"];
    "Stay in this session?" -> "executing-plans" [label="no - parallel session"];
}
```

**vs. Executing Plans (parallel session):**
- Same session (no context switch)
- Fresh subagent per task (no context pollution)
- Two-stage review after each task: spec compliance first, then code quality
- Faster iteration (no human-in-loop between tasks)

## The Process

```dot
digraph process {
    rankdir=TB;

    subgraph cluster_per_task {
        label="Per Task";
        "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Implementer subagent asks questions?" [shape=diamond];
        "Answer questions, provide context" [shape=box];
        "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" [shape=box];
        "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" [shape=diamond];
        "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" [shape=box];
        "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" [shape=diamond];
        "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" [shape=box];
        "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" [shape=box];
    }

    "Read plan, extract all tasks with full text, note context, create TodoWrite" [shape=box];
    "More tasks remain?" [shape=diamond];
    "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" [shape=box];
    "Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch" [shape=box style=filled fillcolor=lightgreen];

    "Read plan, extract all tasks with full text, note context, create TodoWrite" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)";
    "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" -> "Implementer subagent asks questions?";
    "Implementer subagent asks questions?" -> "Answer questions, provide context" [label="yes"];
    "Answer questions, provide context" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)";
    "Implementer subagent asks questions?" -> "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" -> "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)";
    "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" -> "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?";
    "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" -> "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" -> "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="re-review"];
    "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" -> "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="yes"];
    "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" -> "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?";
    "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" -> "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" -> "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="re-review"];
    "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" -> "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" [label="yes"];
    "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" -> "More tasks remain?";
    "More tasks remain?" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" [label="yes"];
    "More tasks remain?" -> "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" [label="no"];
    "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" -> "Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch";
}
```

## Model Selection

Use the least powerful model that can handle each role to conserve cost and increase speed.

**Mechanical implementation tasks** (isolated functions, clear specs, 1-2 files): use a fast, cheap model. Most implementation tasks are mechanical when the plan is well-specified.

**Integration and judgment tasks** (multi-file coordination, pattern matching, debugging): use a standard model.

**Architecture, design, and review tasks**: use the most capable available model.

**Task complexity signals:**
- Touches 1-2 files with a complete spec вЖТ cheap model
- Touches multiple files with integration concerns вЖТ standard model
- Requires design judgment or broad codebase understanding вЖТ most capable model

## Handling Implementer Status

Implementer subagents report one of four statuses. Handle each appropriately:

**DONE:** Proceed to spec compliance review.

**DONE_WITH_CONCERNS:** The implementer completed the work but flagged doubts. Read the concerns before proceeding. If the concerns are about correctness or scope, address them before review. If they're observations (e.g., "this file is getting large"), note them and proceed to review.

**NEEDS_CONTEXT:** The implementer needs information that wasn't provided. Provide the missing context and re-dispatch.

**BLOCKED:** The implementer cannot complete the task. Assess the blocker:
1. If it's a context problem, provide more context and re-dispatch with the same model
2. If the task requires more reasoning, re-dispatch with a more capable model
3. If the task is too large, break it into smaller pieces
4. If the plan itself is wrong, escalate to the human

**Never** ignore an escalation or force the same model to retry without changes. If the implementer said it's stuck, something needs to change.

## Prompt Templates

- `./implementer-prompt.md` - Dispatch implementer subagent
- `./spec-reviewer-prompt.md` - Dispatch spec compliance reviewer subagent
- `./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md` - Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent

## Example Workflow

```
You: I'm using Subagent-Driven Development to execute this plan.

[Read plan file once: docs/superpowers/plans/feature-plan.md]
[Extract all 5 tasks with full text and context]
[Create TodoWrite with all tasks]

Task 1: Hook installation script

[Get Task 1 text and context (already extracted)]
[Dispatch implementation subagent with full task text + context]

Implementer: "Before I begin - should the hook be installed at user or system level?"

You: "User level (~/.config/superpowers/hooks/)"

Implementer: "Got it. Implementing now..."
[Later] Implementer:
  - Implemented install-hook command
  - Added tests, 5/5 passing
  - Self-review: Found I missed --force flag, added it
  - Committed

[Dispatch spec compliance reviewer]
Spec reviewer: вЬЕ Spec compliant - all requirements met, nothing extra

[Get git SHAs, dispatch code quality reviewer]
Code reviewer: Strengths: Good test coverage, clean. Issues: None. Approved.

[Mark Task 1 complete]

Task 2: Recovery modes

[Get Task 2 text and context (already extracted)]
[Dispatch implementation subagent with full task text + context]

Implementer: [No questions, proceeds]
Implementer:
  - Added verify/repair modes
  - 8/8 tests passing
  - Self-review: All good
  - Committed

[Dispatch spec compliance reviewer]
Spec reviewer: вЭМ Issues:
  - Missing: Progress reporting (spec says "report every 100 items")
  - Extra: Added --json flag (not requested)

[Implementer fixes issues]
Implementer: Removed --json flag, added progress reporting

[Spec reviewer reviews again]
Spec reviewer: вЬЕ Spec compliant now

[Dispatch code quality reviewer]
Code reviewer: Strengths: Solid. Issues (Important): Magic number (100)

[Implementer fixes]
Implementer: Extracted PROGRESS_INTERVAL constant

[Code reviewer reviews again]
Code reviewer: вЬЕ Approved

[Mark Task 2 complete]

...

[After all tasks]
[Dispatch final code-reviewer]
Final reviewer: All requirements met, ready to merge

Done!
```

## Advantages

**vs. Manual execution:**
- Subagents follow TDD naturally
- Fresh context per task (no confusion)
- Parallel-safe (subagents don't interfere)
- Subagent can ask questions (before AND during work)

**vs. Executing Plans:**
- Same session (no handoff)
- Continuous progress (no waiting)
- Review checkpoints automatic

**Efficiency gains:**
- No file reading overhead (controller provides full text)
- Controller curates exactly what context is needed
- Subagent gets complete information upfront
- Questions surfaced before work begins (not after)

**Quality gates:**
- Self-review catches issues before handoff
- Two-stage review: spec compliance, then code quality
- Review loops ensure fixes actually work
- Spec compliance prevents over/under-building
- Code quality ensures implementation is well-built

**Cost:**
- More subagent invocations (implementer + 2 reviewers per task)
- Controller does more prep work (extracting all tasks upfront)
- Review loops add iterations
- But catches issues early (cheaper than debugging later)

## Red Flags

**Never:**
- Start implementation on main/master branch without explicit user consent
- Skip reviews (spec compliance OR code quality)
- Proceed with unfixed issues
- Dispatch multiple implementation subagents in parallel (conflicts)
- Make subagent read plan file (provide full text instead)
- Skip scene-setting context (subagent needs to understand where task fits)
- Ignore subagent questions (answer before letting them proceed)
- Accept "close enough" on spec compliance (spec reviewer found issues = not done)
- Skip review loops (reviewer found issues = implementer fixes = review again)
- Let implementer self-review replace actual review (both are needed)
- **Start code quality review before spec compliance is вЬЕ** (wrong order)
- Move to next task while either review has open issues

**If subagent asks questions:**
- Answer clearly and completely
- Provide additional context if needed
- Don't rush them into implementation

**If reviewer finds issues:**
- Implementer (same subagent) fixes them
- Reviewer reviews again
- Repeat until approved
- Don't skip the re-review

**If subagent fails task:**
- Dispatch fix subagent with specific instructions
- Don't try to fix manually (context pollution)

## Integration

**Required workflow skills:**
- **superpowers:using-git-worktrees** - REQUIRED: Set up isolated workspace before starting
- **superpowers:writing-plans** - Creates the plan this skill executes
- **superpowers:requesting-code-review** - Code review template for reviewer subagents
- **superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch** - Complete development after all tasks

**Subagents should use:**
- **superpowers:test-driven-development** - Subagents follow TDD for each task

**Alternative workflow:**
- **superpowers:executing-plans** - Use for parallel session instead of same-session execution

I'm using Subagent-Driven Development to execute this plan.

Let me read the plan once and extract all tasks.

220вЖТ const stored = localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY); 221вЖТ if (stored === "en" || stored === "zh") { 222вЖТ setLocaleState(stored); 223вЖТ document.documentElement.lang = stored; 224вЖТ } 225вЖТ }, []); 226вЖТ 227вЖТ const setLocale = useCallback((next: Locale) => { 228вЖТ setLocaleState(next); 229вЖТ localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, next); 230вЖТ document.documentElement.lang = next; 231вЖТ }, []); 232вЖТ 233вЖТ const t = useCallback( 234вЖТ (key: string): string => { 235вЖТ return messages[locale]?.[key] ?? messages.en?.[key] ?? key; 236вЖТ }, 237вЖТ [locale] 238вЖТ ); 239вЖТ 240вЖТ return ( 241вЖТ 242вЖТ {children} 243вЖТ 244вЖТ ); 245вЖТ} 246вЖТ 247вЖТexport function useTranslation() { 248вЖТ const ctx = useContext(I18nContext); 249вЖТ if (!ctx) throw new Error("useTranslation must be used within LanguageProvider"); 250вЖТ return ctx; 251вЖТ} 252вЖТ``` 253вЖТ 254вЖТ- [ ] **Step 4: Verify TypeScript compiles** 255вЖТ 256вЖТ```bash 257вЖТcd web && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | grep # Implementer Subagent Prompt Template

Use this template when dispatching an implementer subagent.

```
Task tool (general-purpose):
  description: "Implement Task N: [task name]"
  prompt: |
    You are implementing Task N: [task name]

    ## Task Description

    [FULL TEXT of task from plan - paste it here, don't make subagent read file]

    ## Context

    [Scene-setting: where this fits, dependencies, architectural context]

    ## Before You Begin

    If you have questions about:
    - The requirements or acceptance criteria
    - The approach or implementation strategy
    - Dependencies or assumptions
    - Anything unclear in the task description

    **Ask them now.** Raise any concerns before starting work.

    ## Your Job

    Once you're clear on requirements:
    1. Implement exactly what the task specifies
    2. Write tests (following TDD if task says to)
    3. Verify implementation works
    4. Commit your work
    5. Self-review (see below)
    6. Report back

    Work from: [directory]

    **While you work:** If you encounter something unexpected or unclear, **ask questions**.
    It's always OK to pause and clarify. Don't guess or make assumptions.

    ## Code Organization

    You reason best about code you can hold in context at once, and your edits are more
    reliable when files are focused. Keep this in mind:
    - Follow the file structure defined in the plan
    - Each file should have one clear responsibility with a well-defined interface
    - If a file you're creating is growing beyond the plan's intent, stop and report
      it as DONE_WITH_CONCERNS вАФ don't split files on your own without plan guidance
    - If an existing file you're modifying is already large or tangled, work carefully
      and note it as a concern in your report
    - In existing codebases, follow established patterns. Improve code you're touching
      the way a good developer would, but don't restructure things outside your task.

    ## When You're in Over Your Head

    It is always OK to stop and say "this is too hard for me." Bad work is worse than
    no work. You will not be penalized for escalating.

    **STOP and escalate when:**
    - The task requires architectural decisions with multiple valid approaches
    - You need to understand code beyond what was provided and can't find clarity
    - You feel uncertain about whether your approach is correct
    - The task involves restructuring existing code in ways the plan didn't anticipate
    - You've been reading file after file trying to understand the system without progress

    **How to escalate:** Report back with status BLOCKED or NEEDS_CONTEXT. Describe
    specifically what you're stuck on, what you've tried, and what kind of help you need.
    The controller can provide more context, re-dispatch with a more capable model,
    or break the task into smaller pieces.

    ## Before Reporting Back: Self-Review

    Review your work with fresh eyes. Ask yourself:

    **Completeness:**
    - Did I fully implement everything in the spec?
    - Did I miss any requirements?
    - Are there edge cases I didn't handle?

    **Quality:**
    - Is this my best work?
    - Are names clear and accurate (match what things do, not how they work)?
    - Is the code clean and maintainable?

    **Discipline:**
    - Did I avoid overbuilding (YAGNI)?
    - Did I only build what was requested?
    - Did I follow existing patterns in the codebase?

    **Testing:**
    - Do tests actually verify behavior (not just mock behavior)?
    - Did I follow TDD if required?
    - Are tests comprehensive?

    If you find issues during self-review, fix them now before reporting.

    ## Report Format

    When done, report:
    - **Status:** DONE | DONE_WITH_CONCERNS | BLOCKED | NEEDS_CONTEXT
    - What you implemented (or what you attempted, if blocked)
    - What you tested and test results
    - Files changed
    - Self-review findings (if any)
    - Any issues or concerns

    Use DONE_WITH_CONCERNS if you completed the work but have doubts about correctness.
    Use BLOCKED if you cannot complete the task. Use NEEDS_CONTEXT if you need
    information that wasn't provided. Never silently produce work you're unsure about.
```

### Implementation Summary

I successfully implemented dark mode CSS variables for the Changelogue web application:

**Changes Made:**

1. **Added `html.dark` CSS block** (lines 81-115) after the `:root` block with complete dark mode color overrides including:
   - Background, foreground, surface, and layout colors
   - All shadcn component variable mappings

2. **Updated `@theme inline` block** to use CSS variables instead of hardcoded values:
   - Changed `--color-popover: #ffffff` вЖТ `--color-popover: var(--popover)`

**Files Changed:**

**Commit:** `0d40403` - "feat: add dark mode CSS variables"

The dark mode system is now ready to be activated by adding the `.dark` class to the HTML element, allowing all CSS variables to automatically switch to dark theme values.

input.prompt:
You are implementing Task 4: Create Providers wrapper and wire into root layout

## Task Description

**Files:**
- Create: `web/components/providers.tsx`
- Modify: `web/app/layout.tsx`

**Step 1:** Create `web/components/providers.tsx`:

```tsx
"use client";

import { ThemeProvider } from "next-themes";
import { LanguageProvider } from "@/lib/i18n/context";
import type { ReactNode } from "react";

export function Providers({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) {
  return (
    <ThemeProvider
      attribute="class"
      defaultTheme="system"
      enableSystem
      storageKey="changelogue-theme"
    >
      <LanguageProvider>{children}</LanguageProvider>
    </ThemeProvider>
  );
}
```

**Step 2:** Update `web/app/layout.tsx`:

The current file looks like this:
```tsx
import type { Metadata } from "next";
import { Fraunces, DM_Sans } from "next/font/google";
import { AuthProvider } from "@/lib/auth/context";
import { LayoutShell } from "@/components/layout/layout-shell";
import "./globals.css";

// ... fonts ...

export default function RootLayout({ children }) {
  return (
    <html lang="en">
      <body className={`${fraunces.variable} ${dmSans.variable} antialiased`}>
        <AuthProvider>
          <LayoutShell>{children}</LayoutShell>
        </AuthProvider>
      </body>
    </html>
  );
}
```

Changes needed:
1. Add import: `import { Providers } from "@/components/providers";`
2. Change `<html lang="en">` to `<html lang="en" suppressHydrationWarning>`
3. Wrap `<AuthProvider>` inside `<Providers>`:

```tsx
        <Providers>
          <AuthProvider>
            <LayoutShell>{children}</LayoutShell>
          </AuthProvider>
        </Providers>
```

**IMPORTANT:** `layout.tsx` is a Server Component (no "use client"). That's why we created the separate `providers.tsx` as a Client Component. Do NOT add "use client" to layout.tsx. The Server Component can import and render Client Components вАФ it just can't use hooks or client-only APIs itself.

**Step 3:** Commit with message: `feat: wire ThemeProvider and LanguageProvider into root layout via Providers wrapper`

## Context

This is a Next.js 16 (App Router) project at `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon`. The `next-themes` package has been installed (Task 1). The `LanguageProvider` was created in `web/lib/i18n/context.tsx` (Task 3). The `AuthProvider` is already in the layout and must remain inside the Providers wrapper.

## Your Job

1. Read the current layout.tsx
2. Create providers.tsx
3. Modify layout.tsx with the 3 changes above
4. Commit

Work from: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon`

When done, report:
- **Status:** DONE | DONE_WITH_CONCERNS | BLOCKED | NEEDS_CONTEXT

You are implementing Task 6: Update sidebar with DropdownMenu and settings trigger ## Task Description **Files:** - Modify: `web/components/layout/sidebar.tsx` You need to make these changes to the existing sidebar: **Step 1: Add imports** Add these imports to the top of `sidebar.tsx` (alongside the existing imports): ```tsx import { Settings } from "lucide-react"; import { DropdownMenu, DropdownMenuContent, DropdownMenuItem, DropdownMenuSeparator, DropdownMenuTrigger, } from "@/components/ui/dropdown-menu"; import { SettingsDialog } from "@/components/settings/settings-dialog"; import { useTranslation } from "@/lib/i18n/context"; ``` **Step 2: Add state and translation hook** After the existing `const { user, logout } = useAuth();` line inside the Sidebar component, add: ```tsx const [settingsOpen, setSettingsOpen] = useState(false); const { t } = useTranslation(); ``` Note: `useState` is already imported. **Step 3: Refactor sidebar background to use CSS variable** Change the `
` element's style from: ```tsx style={{ backgroundColor: "#16181c" }} ``` to: ```tsx style={{ backgroundColor: "var(--sidebar-bg)" }} ``` **Step 4: Replace the module-level `navItems` array** The current array is: ```tsx const navItems = [ { href: "/", label: "Dashboard", icon: LayoutDashboard }, { href: "/projects", label: "Projects", icon: FolderKanban }, { href: "/todo", label: "Todo", icon: ListTodo }, { href: "/releases", label: "Releases", icon: Package }, { href: "/channels", label: "Channels", icon: Megaphone }, { href: "/subscriptions", label: "Subscriptions", icon: Bell }, ]; ``` Replace with (stays at module scope вАФ only string keys, `t()` is called in JSX): ```tsx const navKeys = [ { href: "/", key: "nav.dashboard", icon: LayoutDashboard }, { href: "/projects", key: "nav.projects", icon: FolderKanban }, { href: "/todo", key: "nav.todo", icon: ListTodo }, { href: "/releases", key: "nav.releases", icon: Package }, { href: "/channels", key: "nav.channels", icon: Megaphone }, { href: "/subscriptions", key: "nav.subscriptions", icon: Bell }, ]; ``` In the nav mapping JSX: - Change `{navItems.map((item) => {` to `{navKeys.map((item) => {` - Change `title={expanded ? undefined : item.label}` to `title={expanded ? undefined : t(item.key)}` - Change `{item.label}` in the `` to `{t(item.key)}` **Step 5: Replace the entire user section** Replace the current `{/* User section */}` block (everything from `{user && (` at the bottom to its closing `)}`) with: ```tsx {/* User section */} {user && (
setSettingsOpen(true)}> {t("user.settings")} {t("user.signout")}
)} ``` **Step 6: Commit with message:** `feat: add settings dropdown to sidebar user menu with i18n labels` ## Context This is a Next.js 16 (App Router) project at `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon`. The sidebar is at `web/components/layout/sidebar.tsx`. It's a "use client" component. Dependencies already set up: - `web/components/ui/dropdown-menu.tsx` exists (shadcn) - `web/components/settings/settings-dialog.tsx` was created (Task 5) - `web/lib/i18n/context.tsx` exists with `useTranslation()` hook (Task 3) - `lucide-react` is installed with Settings icon available ## Your Job 1. Read the current sidebar.tsx 2. Apply all changes exactly as specified 3. Make sure the final file compiles (no syntax errors) 4. Commit Work from: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon` ## Report Format When done, report: - **Status:** DONE | DONE_WITH_CONCERNS | BLOCKED | NEEDS_CONTEXT - What you implemented - Files changed - Self-review findings

The sidebar change is confirmed in the commit (`web/components/layout/sidebar.tsx` shows +98/-39 lines). The commit message was rewritten by the repo's pre-commit tooling (adds an `Entire-Checkpoint` tag and regenerates the message from all staged diffs), but the actual sidebar change is part of `e2831b3`.

---

cd web && npm run dev
```

5. Refresh вАФ both settings should persist

content.text:
Good catches вАФ two issues:

1. **i18n incomplete:** Only sidebar nav labels are translated, but page-level content (tab titles, headings, button labels) still uses hardcoded English
2. **Dark mode incomplete:** Cards, buttons, and other components use hardcoded colors that don't respond to CSS variables

Let me explore the extent of both problems before proposing fixes.

## Context

After implementing the settings dialog with theme toggle (light/dark/system) and language selector (EN/ZH), users reported:
1. **Dark mode broken**: Cards, buttons, tables still show white/light backgrounds because components use hardcoded hex colors instead of CSS variables
2. **i18n incomplete**: Only sidebar nav labels are translated вАФ all page content (titles, tabs, buttons, table headers, messages) remains hardcoded English

This plan systematically replaces all hardcoded colors with CSS variables and adds translation keys across all ~30 affected files.

## Strategy

### Colors: Hardcoded hex вЖТ CSS variable mapping

| Hardcoded hex | Replace with | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `#ffffff`, `bg-white` | `var(--surface)` / `bg-surface` | Card/panel backgrounds |
| `#fafaf9` | `var(--background)` / `bg-background` | Page/section backgrounds |
| `#f3f3f1` | `var(--mono-bg)` / `bg-mono-bg` | Code blocks, badges, muted surfaces |
| `#111113` | `var(--foreground)` / `text-foreground` | Primary text |
| `#374151` | `var(--secondary-foreground)` / `text-secondary-foreground` | Secondary text (darker gray) |
| `#6b7280` | `var(--text-secondary)` / `text-text-secondary` | Secondary text |
| `#9ca3af` | `var(--text-muted)` / `text-text-muted` | Muted/placeholder text |
| `#b0b0a8`, `#c4c4c0` | `var(--text-muted)` | Light icons/decorative |
| `#e8e8e5` | `var(--border)` / `border-border` | Borders |
| `#e8601a` | `var(--beacon-accent)` | Accent (same in both themes) |
| `#fff7ed` | `var(--beacon-accent)` with `/10` opacity | Light accent surfaces |
| `#f8f8f6` | `var(--background)` | Auth loading background |
| `rgba(107,114,128,0.08)` | `var(--muted)` | Subtle badges |

For inline styles: use `var(--variable-name)`
For Tailwind classes: use `bg-surface`, `text-foreground`, `border-border`, etc.

### i18n: Add keys to en.json/zh.json, use `t()` in components

Group translation keys by page/component. Components that need `t()` must be client components (add `"use client"` if missing, or pass translated strings as props from client parent).

## Execution: Parallel subagents by file group

Since the changes are mechanical (find/replace patterns), dispatch subagents in parallel by file group. Each subagent gets a batch of related files.

### Batch 1: Core layout + shared components (parallel)

**Agent A вАФ Header + Auth context + shared UI components:**
- `web/components/layout/header.tsx` вАФ hardcoded colors + English labels
- `web/lib/auth/context.tsx` вАФ `bg-[#f8f8f6]` loading state
- `web/components/ui/section-label.tsx` вАФ `text-[#9ca3af]`
- `web/components/ui/version-chip.tsx` вАФ hardcoded colors
- `web/components/ui/urgency-callout.tsx` вАФ `text-[#374151]`

**Agent B вАФ Dashboard components (part 1):**
- `web/app/page.tsx` вАФ dashboard page colors + English text
- `web/components/dashboard/stats-cards.tsx` вАФ card borders, text colors, English labels
- `web/components/dashboard/empty-state.tsx` вАФ all hardcoded colors + English text
- `web/components/dashboard/release-trend-chart.tsx` вАФ border + English label
- `web/components/dashboard/activity-feed.tsx` вАФ English labels + colors

**Agent C вАФ Dashboard components (part 2):**
- `web/components/dashboard/suggestions-section.tsx` вАФ borders, tab text, English labels
- `web/components/dashboard/unified-feed.tsx` вАФ borders, icon colors
- `web/components/dashboard/discovery-section.tsx` вАФ extensive hardcoded colors

### Batch 2: Dashboard scan/deps + onboard (parallel)

**Agent D вАФ Scan/deps tabs:**
- `web/components/dashboard/deps-tab.tsx` вАФ extensive hardcoded colors + English text
- `web/components/dashboard/scan-url-tab.tsx` вАФ same patterns
- `web/components/dashboard/stars-tab.tsx` вАФ loading/error messages
- `web/components/dashboard/shared/scan-results-table.tsx` вАФ table headers + colors
- `web/components/dashboard/shared/ecosystem-colors.ts` вАФ check if needs dark variants

**Agent E вАФ Onboard page:**
- `web/app/onboard/page.tsx` вАФ extensive hardcoded colors + English text (standalone page)

### Batch 3: Full pages (parallel)

**Agent F вАФ Releases + channels pages:**
- `web/app/releases/page.tsx` вАФ extensive colors + English text
- `web/app/channels/page.tsx` вАФ borders, colors, dialog labels
- `web/components/releases/release-detail.tsx` вАФ hardcoded colors throughout

**Agent G вАФ Projects + subscriptions + other pages:**
- `web/app/projects/page.tsx` вАФ colors + dialog labels
- `web/app/subscriptions/page.tsx` вАФ colors + dialog labels
- `web/app/todo/page.tsx` вАФ tab labels + colors
- `web/app/sources/page.tsx` вАФ table headers + colors
- `web/components/projects/project-detail.tsx` вАФ extensive hardcoded colors

### Batch 4: Remaining components + globals.css release-notes

**Agent H вАФ Misc components + release notes CSS:**
- `web/components/agent/agent-page.tsx` вАФ gray badge
- `web/components/dashboard/activity-feed.tsx` вАФ if not done in batch 1
- `web/components/releases/semantic-releases-list.tsx` вАФ bg-white
- `web/app/globals.css` вАФ `.release-notes-content` block dark overrides

### Batch 5: i18n message files update

**Agent I вАФ Update en.json and zh.json with all new keys:**
- Collect all translation keys from batches 1-4
- Update `web/lib/i18n/messages/en.json` with all new keys
- Update `web/lib/i18n/messages/zh.json` with Chinese translations

Note: This must run AFTER batches 1-4 so we know the full set of keys. However, each batch agent should define what keys they need, and Agent I consolidates.

Actually вАФ **simpler approach:** Each batch agent adds their own keys to en.json/zh.json as they go. Later batches just append. No coordination needed since JSON keys don't conflict.

## Key Rules for All Agents

1. **Colors:** Replace hardcoded hex with CSS variables per the mapping table above
2. **Tailwind classes:** Use semantic tokens (`bg-surface`, `text-foreground`, `border-border`, `bg-muted`, `text-muted-foreground`) instead of arbitrary values
3. **Inline styles:** Use `var(--variable-name)` syntax
4. **i18n:** Add translation keys to en.json/zh.json, use `t()` from `useTranslation()` hook
5. **Client components:** If a Server Component needs `t()`, either make it a client component or pass translated strings as props
6. **Don't break layout:** Same visual appearance in light mode, proper theming in dark mode
7. **Commit after each batch** with descriptive message

## Verification

After all batches:
1. `cd web && npx tsc --noEmit` вАФ TypeScript compiles clean
2. `cd web && npm run dev` вАФ dev server starts
3. Toggle dark mode вАФ all pages should have dark backgrounds, light text, no white cards
4. Switch to дЄ≠жЦЗ вАФ all user-facing text on every page should be Chinese
5. Switch back to English вАФ all text reverts
6. Check each page: Dashboard, Projects, Releases, Channels, Subscriptions, Todo, Sources, Onboard
